title: Podman machine user UID on WSL collides on another WSL user when systemd is enabled
body:
### Issue Description

Basically what I've also reported [here](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/13985#issuecomment-3691527973), on WSL repo. Not sure if it's a podman problem or a WSL problem.

When systemd is enabled, `podman-machine` default VM has the same UID as the "user WSL". The `user@1000.service` than fails to start, and the `podman-user.sock` is not created, breaking `podman-remote` in rootless mode. 

I tried changing the UID of the "work WSL" (i.e. not the `podman-machine-default`), but now I'm experiencing permission problems on the exported socket (because that one has UID 1000). 

```
❯ ls -al /mnt/wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default
total 0
drwxrwxrwx 2 root root 80 Dec 28 00:37 .
drwxrwxrwx 3 root root 60 Dec 28 00:37 ..
srw-rw---- 1 root   10  0 Dec 28 00:37 podman-root.sock
srw-rw---- 1 1000 1000  0 Dec 28 00:37 podman-user.sock
❯ id
```

I don't understand why two machines interfere with each other systemd processes tho. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Described in this other [issue](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/27763#issuecomment-3650460017).



### Describe the results you received

Based on which VMs is started first, the `user@1000.service` is not started correctly for the second macine running.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output


From my Arch WSL: 

```
Cannot connect to Podman. Please verify your connection to the Linux system using `podman system connection list`, or try `podman machine init` and `podman machine start` to manage a new Linux VM
Error: unable to connect to Podman socket: Get "http://d/v5.7.1/libpod/_ping": dial unix /mnt/wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default/podman-user.sock: connect: permission denied: unix:///mnt/wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default/podman-user.sock
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

- Podman installed using `scoop` in the Windows host. 
- Podman also installed using `pacman` in the "work" WSL, with `CONTAINER_HOST=unix:///mnt/wsl/podman-sockets/podman-machine-default/podman-user.sock`

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
